claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children. I'm inquiring about both of them - Alex, who's 8, and Blake, who's 14.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have two children - Alex (age 8) and Blake (age 14).

Now, let me ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And do both Alex and Blake live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, in care, or in residential accommodation for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in hospital, care, or residential accommodation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One more question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both of your children.**

**For Alex (age 8):**
You qualify because Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you're responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and they're not in hospital or care.

**For Blake (age 14):**
You qualify because Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you're responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and they're not in hospital or care.

Since both children live with you and are under 16, you meet all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

**Important things to note:**
- Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child
- You may need to be aware of the High Income Child Benefit tax charge if you or your partner earn over £60,000 individually
- When Blake turns 16, you can continue to receive Child Benefit if they stay in approved education or training until they're under 20

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for both children!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16. Alex lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Alex as they live with you. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 14 years old, which is under 16. Blake lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Blake as they live with you. Blake is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with both children (Alex, age 8, and Blake, age 14). Both children are under 16 years old and live with the claimant, making the claimant responsible for them. Neither child is in hospital, care, or residential accommodation, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.

Blake is 14 years old, which is under 16. Blake lives with you in the UK. You are responsible for Blake as they live with you. Blake is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.

Both children are under 16 years old and live with the claimant, making the claimant responsible for them. Neither child is in hospital, care, or residential accommodation, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.
